sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Western Digital has exhausted its HDD production capacity for the entire year of 2026 due to the AI boom
feed.summary: Western Digital has completely sold out its HDD production capacity for the entire year of 2026, driven primarily by the high demand from AI hyperscalers and corporate clients. The cloud segment now accounts for 89% of the company's total revenue, with HDDs remaining the most economical solution for large-scale data storage required by AI. This early booking in February 2026 highlights the significant impact of the AI boom on data storage infrastructure.

feed.title: S&P Global: An Undervalued Dividend King For Long-Term Investors (SPGI)
feed.summary: S&P Global (SPGI) is highlighted as an undervalued Dividend King provider of credit ratings, benchmarks, and analytics. The article notes its 53-year dividend growth streak and strong financial position with a low debt/equity ratio and high interest coverage. It suggests SPGI's current valuation makes it an attractive long-term investment despite potential risks.

feed.title: Consumers Energy Promotes Home Heating Credit to Help Customers Pay Energy Bills – Company Announcement
feed.summary: Consumers Energy is encouraging its customers to apply for the Michigan Home Heating Credit this tax season, which provides an average benefit of nearly $200 to eligible households. This initiative aims to help families manage rising costs, especially after a recent cold snap led to higher energy bills. The company is also providing $5 million to nonprofit organizations for energy bill assistance and reaching out to customers most affected by the cold to help with payments and energy reduction.

feed.title: L3Harris To Begin Modification Work on Korean AEW&C Global 6500s
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies has begun modification work on the first two of four Bombardier Global Express 6500 business jets for the Republic of Korea Air Force. These aircraft will be missionized for Airborne Early Warning and Control (AEW&C) purposes. The company is integrating mission systems on these jets, which will ultimately serve as crucial assets for South Korea's airborne surveillance capabilities.

feed.title: VanEck Natural Resources Fund Approaches Peak Ahead of Portfolio Reshuffle
feed.summary: The VanEck Natural Resources ETF is nearing its 52-week high due to robust global demand for industrial and energy commodities. Investors are keenly awaiting next month's portfolio rebalancing, which will adjust holdings based on market performance, with a focus on target allocations for energy, metals, agriculture, and sustainable resources. The fund utilizes a global investment strategy and a tiered weighting methodology to balance traditional and renewable segments, distinguishing itself from competitors.

feed.title: Here's How to Play Kinross Gold Stock Before Q4 Earnings Release
feed.summary: Kinross Gold Corporation (KGC) is set to report its Q4 2025 earnings on February 18, with analysts expecting a significant year-over-year increase in earnings and revenue, driven by higher gold prices. The company is predicted to beat earnings expectations, boasting a positive Earnings ESP and a Zacks Rank #3. Despite strong performance and a promising pipeline of projects, Kinross faces headwinds from rising production costs, which could impact profit margins.

feed.title: RTX Corporation Stock (RTX) Opinions on Quarterly Earnings Beat
feed.summary: RTX Corporation reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings, leading to investor optimism due to strong defense spending and a solid backlog in aerospace and missiles. Despite some concerns about supply chain disruptions, the overall market sentiment highlighted the company's resilience and strategic advantages in advanced weaponry. Analysts have largely issued "Buy" ratings, with a median price target of $211.5, suggesting shares are attractively priced post-rally.

feed.title: Virginie Helias: Community Impact at Procter & Gamble
feed.summary: Virginie Helias, Procter & Gamble's Chief Sustainability Officer, has been recognized among the top sustainability leaders for her 37-year career dedicated to embedding sustainability into the company's operations. Her focus includes reducing environmental impact and supporting communities through health and hygiene initiatives. She will speak at Sustainability LIVE: The Net Zero Summit about the evolving role of CSOs.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Announces a Flock of Animal-Friendly Festivities Launching This Month
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company is launching a series of animal-focused events in February, including their annual Chick Days, the new Animal Days savings celebration, and a nationwide Farm Bash. These events offer savings, supplies, and activities for pet and animal owners, with the Farm Bash celebrating the start of Animal Days on February 21st in stores across the country.

feed.title: Microsoft Teams class action claims company illegally collects voice data
feed.summary: Five Illinois residents have filed a class action lawsuit against Microsoft Corp., alleging that its Microsoft Teams software illegally collects and analyzes users' voice data without proper notice. The lawsuit claims that the software's real-time transcription feature uses "diarization" to create "voiceprints" from users' voices, violating the Illinois Biometric Privacy Act (BIPA). The plaintiffs are seeking actual damages or $1,000 per negligent violation, potentially rising to $5,000 for intentional or reckless violations.

feed.title: Preview: Vulcan Materials's Earnings
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials (NYSE: VMC) is set to announce its quarterly earnings on February 17, 2026, with analysts forecasting an EPS of $2.12. Investors are keen to see if the company surpasses estimates and provides positive guidance for the next quarter, as market reactions are often driven more by guidance than past performance. The company's stock has seen a 20.12% increase over the past 52 weeks, demonstrating positive returns for long-term shareholders.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp's (NYSE:USB) institutional investors lost 4.4% last week but have benefitted from longer-term gains
feed.summary: Institutional investors own 82% of U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) and experienced a 4.4% loss last week, though they have seen a 26% return over the past year. The Vanguard Group is the largest shareholder with 9.4% ownership, and the top 23 investors collectively hold 50% of the company. Insiders own less than 1% of the shares, while the general public holds a 17% stake.

feed.title: CONAGRA BRANDS REAFFIRMS FISCAL 2026 GUIDANCE AHEAD OF 2026 CAGNY PRESENTATION
feed.summary: Conagra Brands Inc. is reaffirming its fiscal 2026 guidance ahead of its presentation at the 2026 Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) conference. The company expects organic net sales change of (1)% to 1% compared to fiscal 2025, adjusted operating margin between ~11.0% and ~11.5%, and adjusted EPS between $1.70 and $1.85. Additionally, Conagra now anticipates free cash flow conversion to be approximately 100% for the full year, an increase from its previous expectation.

feed.title: O'Reilly Automotive's (NASDAQ:ORLY) five-year earnings growth trails the 26% YoY shareholder returns
feed.summary: O'Reilly Automotive's stock price has seen an impressive 217% increase over the last five years, outperforming its earnings per share (EPS) growth of 14% per year during the same period. This suggests that the market holds the company in high regard, anticipating continued growth. While CEO remuneration is reasonable, investors are encouraged to further examine the company's fundamentals for future growth potential.

feed.title: New 765-kV power lines could serve 2M homes near Columbus
feed.summary: FirstEnergy Transmission and Transource Energy have received approval from the PJM Board to jointly develop a major electric transmission project in central Ohio. This project will include over 300 miles of new 765-kV lines and substation upgrades, aiming to enhance reliability, support growing demand in the Columbus region, and attract new industry. The collaboration, through Grid Growth Ventures LLC, is designed to reduce land use and deliver cost-effective solutions for the region's increasing power needs.

feed.title: Lloyds Banking Group - Publication of a Supplementary Prospectus
feed.summary: Lloyds Banking Group plc has announced the publication of a new Supplementary Prospectus dated February 16, 2026, which has been approved by the Financial Conduct Authority. This document relates to their £25,000,000,000 Euro Medium Term Note Programme and should be read alongside previous prospectuses. The announcement also includes contact information for investor relations, corporate treasury, and corporate affairs, and specifies important disclaimers regarding intended addressees and securities distribution.

feed.title: Icon PLC headcount plummets 1,800 in a year amid ‘strong cost control’
feed.summary: The article reports that Icon PLC's headcount has decreased by 1,800 over the past year due to "strong cost control" measures. This significant reduction suggests a strategic effort to manage expenses and optimize operations within the company.

feed.title: AI-run Wi‑Fi 7 to manage crowds, cut power use at Atlético’s stadium
feed.summary: Atlético de Madrid is modernizing its Riyadh Air Metropolitano Stadium with HPE Networking technology, implementing an AI-driven Wi-Fi 7 network. This upgrade includes over 1,500 AI-managed access points to optimize performance based on crowd density, reduce energy consumption, and enhance the fan experience through advanced analytics and location services. The project also extends to new sports facilities and a High-Performance Center, positioning the stadium at the forefront of technological innovation in sports.

feed.title: Liberia sees iron ore output tripling to 30 million tons on ArcelorMittal expansion
feed.summary: Liberia anticipates a significant increase in its iron ore production, projecting a rise to 30 million tons following the expansion of ArcelorMittal's operations. This expansion is expected to nearly triple the country's current output. The growth in iron ore exports could have a substantial impact on Liberia's economy.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan Rose 47% in the Last 6 Months. Here’s How High the Stock Could Climb in 2026
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan stock has risen 47% in the last six months due to strengthening copper prices and improving production forecasts. The company's adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was nearly $10 billion, and analysts expect further revenue growth and margin expansion. Based on TIKR's valuation model, the stock could reach $97, implying a 54% upside.

feed.title: Reflective Coating Market Booming Rapidly With Top Key Players
feed.summary: Coherent Market Insights has published a comprehensive study on the "Reflective Coating Market," detailing industry trends, share, size, growth opportunities, and forecasts for 2026-2033. The report includes market dynamics, competitive landscape analysis with key players like PPG Industries and Akzo Nobel, and regional assessments, aiming to provide strategic insights for informed business decisions. It segments the market by type (Acrylic, Silicone, Polyurethane) and applications (Roofing, Automotive, Industrial Equipment), supported by rigorous research methodology.

feed.title: Should Brown & Brown’s (BRO) New US$250 Million Buyback Shape How Investors View Its Capital Strategy?
feed.summary: Brown & Brown (BRO) recently announced a new US$250 million accelerated share repurchase, building on a previous US$622.96 million buyback. This move, part of a larger US$1.5 billion authorization, reinforces the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders. While buybacks indicate financial health, the article emphasizes that investors should also consider the company's execution on acquisitions and the market's evolving insurance pricing and claims trends as key factors influencing its investment narrative.

feed.title: Tyler Technologies (TYL) Is Down 14.4% After 2026 Outlook And New US$1 Billion Buyback Announcement
feed.summary: Tyler Technologies (TYL) experienced a 14.4% stock drop following its 2026 outlook, despite reporting strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results with revenue increases and significant net income. The company announced a new US$1 billion share repurchase program and highlighted advancements in AI and cloud transitions, but investors are weighing this against slower projected growth. The article suggests that the buyback, combined with the growth guidance, reshapes the investment narrative for TYL, particularly concerning capital allocation and the potential impact of government budget caution.

feed.title: Mon Power and Potomac Edison Select Maidsville, West Virginia, Site for New Natural Gas Power Plant
feed.summary: Mon Power and Potomac Edison, subsidiaries of FirstEnergy Corp., have chosen a 35-acre site in Maidsville, West Virginia, adjacent to their Fort Martin Power Station, for a new 1,200-megawatt natural gas power plant. This plant, expected to power half a million homes, is a key part of the companies' Integrated Resource Plan to ensure reliable and affordable electricity for West Virginia. The plan also includes continuing operations at existing power plants and adding 70 megawatts of new solar generation on former industrial and reclaimed mine lands.

feed.title: Have Lattice Semiconductor Insiders Been Selling Stock?
feed.summary: An independent director at Lattice Semiconductor (NASDAQ:LSCC) recently sold a significant amount of stock, though it represented a small portion of their total holding. While there was a large insider purchase last year by the CEO, it was at a much lower price than the current stock value. Overall, insider selling has outpaced buying over the past year, and the article notes some "warning signs" for the company.

feed.title: Canceling elections won’t lower Alabama power bills
feed.summary: A proposed bill in Alabama aims to eliminate popular elections for the Public Service Commission (PSC) and switch to an appointed board, with proponents claiming this would lower utility bills due to more experienced commissioners. The author argues against this, suggesting that "expertise doesn't guarantee good outcomes" and that the real issue behind high Alabama power bills is a "rubber stamp" rate-setting process rather than the democratic election of commissioners. The article highlights that PSC elections are the only public voice in rate decisions and suggests the rush to end elections could be a response to recent challenges against incumbent commissioners and wins by affordability-focused candidates in other states.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Appoints John W. Chidsey as President and Chief Executive Officer
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NCLH) has appointed John W. Chidsey as its new President and Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately. Chidsey, previously CEO of Subway Restaurants and a director of NCLH, replaces Harry Sommer. The company expects its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results to align with previous guidance and will release further details on March 2, 2026.

feed.title: RTX Advances Drone Defense with Reusable Counter-System Technology
feed.summary: RTX, through its Raytheon subsidiary, is developing advanced reusable counter-drone technology, "Coyote Block 3 Non-Kinetic" system, which successfully neutralized drone swarms using non-kinetic effectors. This innovation comes as the company reports strong financial performance, with significant revenue and EPS growth, alongside increased institutional investment. However, market participants are also observing recent insider stock sales by company executives.

feed.title: Brown & Brown announces appointment of Dorothea (Dori) Henderson as chief information technology officer
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. has appointed Dorothea "Dori" Henderson as chief information technology officer, effective February 23, 2026. She will be responsible for leading the company's enterprise technology strategy, enhancing digital capabilities, and overseeing initiatives to improve the technology experience for teammates and customers. Henderson's role will involve working with various leaders to enhance tools, drive data and AI strategy, bolster system security, and support technology integration for acquisitions.

feed.title: Ross Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:ROST) Short Interest Update
feed.summary: Ross Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:ROST) experienced a 15.3% decrease in short interest in January, with 7,564,612 shares shorted as of January 30th, representing about 2.4% of total shares and a 3.0-day short-interest ratio. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $189.94, and the company recently surpassed quarterly earnings expectations and provided positive FY2025 guidance. Institutional investors hold a significant portion of the stock, with 86.86% owned by hedge funds and other institutional investors.

feed.title: EQT Corporation (NYSE:EQT) Short Interest Down 15.5% in January
feed.summary: Short interest in EQT Corporation (NYSE:EQT) decreased by 15.5% in January, totaling 22,156,524 shares, which represents 3.6% of shares sold short and a short-interest ratio of 1.6 days. The company recently announced a quarterly dividend of $0.165, payable on March 2nd. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for EQT with an average price target of $64.27.

feed.title: Final weeks before beloved Olive Garden sister restaurant closes down every single location for good
feed.summary: Bahama Breeze, a sister restaurant to Olive Garden owned by Darden Restaurants, is closing all of its 28 locations by April 5th after 30 years in business. Half of these will be converted into other Darden brands over the next 12-18 months, while the remaining 14 will close permanently across nine states. Darden Restaurants stated that Bahama Breeze is no longer considered a "strategic priority."

feed.title: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish on ServiceNow Stock?
feed.summary: ServiceNow (NOW) stock has significantly underperformed the broader market and the S&P Software & Services ETF over the past year, despite a recent surge. Analysts anticipate strong EPS growth for fiscal 2026 and have a consensus "Strong Buy" rating, with potential upsides suggesting it may be undervalued. This comes after the SaaS sector experienced a downturn, but institutional investors are returning to established companies with recurring revenue.

feed.title: Zions Bancorporation National Association UT Buys 9,452 Shares of Vertex Pharmaceuticals Incorporated $VRTX
feed.summary: Zions Bancorporation National Association UT significantly increased its stake in Vertex Pharmaceuticals, purchasing 9,452 shares to hold a total of 9,638 shares valued at approximately $3.78 million. Vertex Pharmaceuticals reported Q4 EPS of $5.03 and revenues of $3.19 billion, exceeding most forecasts, leading to multiple analyst price target increases and a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating. Despite recent insider selling, institutional investors hold a substantial 90.96% of the stock.

feed.title: M&G PLC Purchases 29,036 Shares of Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. $RCL
feed.summary: M&G PLC increased its stake in Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. by 17.7% in Q3, purchasing 29,036 additional shares and now owning 193,493 shares valued at approximately $62.7 million. This comes despite significant insider selling by CEO Michael W. Bayley and CEO Jason T. Liberty, who together offloaded 170,910 shares worth nearly $56 million. The company reported strong Q4 results with increased revenue and EPS, authorized a $2.0 billion share buyback, and raised its quarterly dividend, though it also issued $2.5 billion in senior notes and reported rising capital expenditures.

feed.title: Vanguard Group Inc. Acquires 272,828 Shares of Stryker Corporation $SYK
feed.summary: Vanguard Group Inc. increased its stake in Stryker Corporation by 0.8% in the third quarter, acquiring an additional 272,828 shares, bringing its total holding to 33,773,399 shares valued at approximately $12.49 billion. This makes Vanguard one of the largest institutional holders of Stryker stock, owning about 8.83% of the company. The article also details recent insider selling, analyst ratings, and Stryker's strong quarterly earnings beat and dividend announcement.

feed.title: Texas Yale Capital Corp. Has $4.83 Million Stake in Merck & Co., Inc. $MRK
feed.summary: Texas Yale Capital Corp. significantly increased its stake in Merck & Co., Inc. by 51.7% in the third quarter, bringing its total holdings to 57,483 shares valued at $4.83 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Merck, which reported strong quarterly earnings of $2.04 EPS on $16.4 billion revenue and declared a $0.85 quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $123.71, despite some insider stock sales totaling around $38.3 million.

feed.title: Zions Bancorporation National Association UT Sells 15,572 Shares of Monster Beverage Corporation $MNST
feed.summary: Zions Bancorporation National Association UT reduced its stake in Monster Beverage Corporation by 18.8% in Q3, selling 15,572 shares and retaining 67,102 shares valued at $4.52 million. Other institutional investors like Norges Bank and Schroder Investment Management Group increased their holdings, with institutional ownership now at 72.36%. Significant insider selling also occurred in December, with two directors selling substantial shares.

feed.title: Vanguard Group Inc. Buys 163,556 Shares of Medtronic PLC $MDT
feed.summary: Vanguard Group Inc. has increased its stake in Medtronic PLC by 0.1% in the third quarter, acquiring 163,556 additional shares to now own a total of 127,072,962 shares, valued at approximately $12.10 billion. This increase comes as Medtronic receives positive analyst sentiment and upgrades following the FDA clearance of its Stealth AXiS system for spine surgery and strong financial performance, including beating quarterly earnings estimates and providing solid FY2026 guidance. The company maintains a "Moderate Buy" analyst consensus with an average price target of $111.21 and offers a dividend yield of around 2.9%.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin Corporation $LMT Shares Sold by Vanguard Group Inc.
feed.summary: Vanguard Group Inc. reduced its stake in Lockheed Martin Corporation by 1.9% in the third quarter, selling 408,877 shares, though still holding 9.20% of the company worth over $10.6 billion. Other institutional investors showed mixed activity, with some increasing and others initiating stakes. Lockheed Martin recently reported quarterly EPS of $5.80, missing consensus, but revenue rose 9.1% to $20.32 billion, and the company announced a quarterly dividend of $3.45 per share.

feed.title: eBay price buoyed by pivotal support - Forecast today - 16-02-2026
feed.summary: Economies.com forecasts a bearish outlook for eBay (EBAY) stock today, despite a limited rise and positive short-term momentum. The stock remains under negative pressure below its 50-day SMA and a broken upward trend line. The forecast suggests a decline, especially if the $79.80 support level is breached, targeting $72.85.

feed.title: M&G PLC Acquires 2,221 Shares of Equinix, Inc. $EQIX
feed.summary: M&G PLC increased its stake in Equinix, Inc. by 0.7%, acquiring an additional 2,221 shares, bringing its total holding to 324,746 shares, representing approximately 1.3% of M&G's investment portfolio. Despite missing Q4 earnings and revenue estimates, Equinix's stock rose due to strong 2026 guidance driven by AI-related bookings, prompting several analysts to raise their price targets and re-rate the stock as "buy" or "overweight." The company also announced a 10% increase in its quarterly dividend to $5.16 per share, while institutional ownership remains high at 94.94%.

feed.title: Vanguard Group Inc. Grows Stake in Blackstone Inc. $BX
feed.summary: Vanguard Group Inc. increased its stake in Blackstone Inc. by 1.9% in the third quarter, bringing its total holdings to 70,827,437 shares valued at approximately $12.1 billion. This increase follows Blackstone beating its recent earnings estimates with an EPS of $1.75 and revenue of $4.36 billion, and the company also announced an increased quarterly dividend of $1.49 per share. Other institutional investors have also modified their holdings, and analysts currently rate Blackstone with a "Moderate Buy" consensus.

feed.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Company $BMY Shares Acquired by M&G PLC
feed.summary: M&G PLC increased its stake in Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) by 1.4%, making it their ninth-largest holding, following institutional buying from other major firms. Despite missing EPS expectations, BMY exceeded revenue estimates in its latest quarterly report and raised its quarterly dividend. Analyst ratings for BMY are mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating but some firms raising price targets and issuing "Buy" ratings.

feed.title: Zions Bancorporation National Association UT Has $8.40 Million Stock Holdings in U.S. Bancorp $USB
feed.summary: Zions Bancorporation National Association UT reduced its stake in U.S. Bancorp (USB) by 12.5% in the third quarter, holding $8.40 million worth of shares. Meanwhile, an insider sold shares worth $1.95 million, decreasing his holding by nearly 25%. U.S. Bancorp recently exceeded quarterly earnings and revenue estimates and pays a $0.52 quarterly dividend, resulting in a Moderate Buy consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $59.79.

feed.title: M&G PLC Increases Position in UnitedHealth Group Incorporated $UNH
feed.summary: M&G PLC increased its stake in UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (NYSE:UNH) by 0.6% during the third quarter, bringing its total ownership to 875,713 shares valued at $302.1 million. This makes UnitedHealth Group the 10th largest holding in M&G PLC's portfolio. Despite some recent price target reductions by Wall Street analysts and concerns about flat Medicare Advantage rates and medical inflation, the consensus rating for UNH remains a "Moderate Buy" with a target price of $372.13.

feed.title: AbbVie Inc. $ABBV Stake Cut by Zions Bancorporation National Association UT
feed.summary: Zions Bancorporation National Association UT reduced its stake in AbbVie Inc. (NYSE:ABBV) by 14.3% in the third quarter, selling 9,468 shares and retaining 56,598 shares valued at $13.105 million. Despite this, AbbVie reported strong Q4 results with record net sales, exceeding revenue and EPS estimates, and provided robust FY2026 EPS guidance. However, the company faces risks, including a lawsuit against the HHS regarding Medicare price negotiations for Botox, which introduces legal uncertainty and has led some analysts to trim price targets, resulting in a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating.

feed.title: AbbVie Inc. $ABBV Shares Sold by M&G PLC
feed.summary: M&G PLC reduced its stake in AbbVie Inc. by 7.8% in Q3, selling 22,406 shares, though institutional ownership remains high at 70.23%. This comes as AbbVie beat quarterly earnings estimates and issued strong FY2026 EPS guidance, maintaining a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts, despite facing legal risks over Medicare drug-price negotiations for Botox. Other major institutions like Norges Bank, Vanguard Group Inc., and Laurel Wealth Advisors LLC significantly increased their positions in the company.

feed.title: Vanguard Group Inc. Trims Stock Position in Honeywell International Inc. $HON
feed.summary: Vanguard Group Inc. reduced its stake in Honeywell International Inc. by 0.3% in the third quarter, now holding 62,134,318 shares valued at approximately $13.08 billion. Despite this, Honeywell recorded strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations with an EPS of $2.59 and revenue of $10.07 billion. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $244.50, and recent insider activity showed a VP selling 5,274 shares.

feed.title: Humana Inc. $HUM Shares Sold by Tobam
feed.summary: Tobam has significantly reduced its stake in Humana Inc. (NYSE:HUM) by 36.2%, selling 6,754 shares and now holding 11,910 shares valued at $3.10 million. This move comes as institutional investors collectively own about 92.4% of the stock amidst growing analyst concern over Medicare Advantage cost pressures and a cautious 2026 outlook, leading to lowered price targets and a consensus "Hold" rating. Despite these challenges, Humana is expanding its value-based primary care footprint, particularly through its CenterWell division, which could serve as a long-term growth catalyst.
